---
title: "Core Concepts in Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications"
description: "Synthesis note on recurring concepts in Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications."
book_id: "evaluation-theory-models-applications"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Synthesis"
citation_key: "stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch01-chapter-1-overview-of-the-evaluation-field:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch02-chapter-2-evaluation-theory:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch03-chapter-3-standards-for-program-evaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch04-chapter-4-background-for-assessing-evaluation-approaches:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch05-chapter-5-pseudoevaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch06-chapter-6-quasi-evaluation-studies:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:improvement-and-accountability-oriented-evaluation-defined:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:overview-of-social-agenda-and-advocacy-approaches:c0003"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:approach-22-utilization-focused-evaluation:c0002"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch10-chapter-10-best-approaches-for-twenty-first-century-evaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:chapter-overview:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch12-chapter-12-case-study-evaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch13-chapter-13-daniel-stufflebeam-s-cipp-model-for-evaluation-an-improvement-and-acc:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch14-chapter-14-michael-scriven-s-consumer-oriented-approach-to-evaluation:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch15-chapter-15-robert-stake-s-responsive-or-stakeholder-centered-evaluation-approach:c0002"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch16-chapter-16-michael-patton-s-utilization-focused-evaluation:c0003"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch17-chapter-17-identifying-and-assessing-evaluation-opportunities:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch18-chapter-18-first-steps-in-addressing-evaluation-opportunities:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch19-chapter-19-designing-evaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch20-chapter-20-budgeting-evaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch21-chapter-21-contracting-evaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch22-chapter-22-collecting-evaluative-information:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch23-chapter-23-analyzing-and-synthesizing-information:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch24-chapter-24-communicating-evaluation-findings:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch25-chapter-25-metaevaluation-evaluating-evaluations:c0001"
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:ch26-chapter-26-institutionalizing-and-mainstreaming-evaluation:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

This synthesis draws across the chapter notes for *Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications* [@stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications].

## Central Logic

The book treats evaluation as a standards-guided practice for producing defensible judgments of value and helping users improve programs, make decisions, and meet accountability obligations.

## Concepts

### Value and Judgment

- merit and worth: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- probity: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- value criteria: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- justified conclusions: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- consumer needs: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

### Standards and Accountability

- utility: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- feasibility: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- propriety: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- accuracy: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- evaluation accountability: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- metaevaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

### Approach Families

- pseudoevaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- quasi-evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- improvement and accountability: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- social agenda and advocacy: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- eclectic evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

### Selected Models

- CIPP: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- consumer-oriented evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- responsive evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- utilization-focused evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- case study evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- experimental design evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

### Evaluation Process

- opportunity assessment: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- evaluation design: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- budgeting: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- contracting: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- information collection: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- analysis and synthesis: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
- communication and use: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

## Links

- [Chapter 1: Overview of the Evaluation Field](chapter-01-overview-of-the-evaluation-field.qmd)
- [Chapter 2: Evaluation Theory](chapter-02-evaluation-theory.qmd)
- [Chapter 3: Standards for Program Evaluations](chapter-03-standards-for-program-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 4: Background for Assessing Evaluation Approaches](chapter-04-background-for-assessing-evaluation-approaches.qmd)
- [Chapter 5: Pseudoevaluations](chapter-05-pseudoevaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 6: Quasi-evaluation Studies](chapter-06-quasi-evaluation-studies.qmd)
- [Chapter 7: Improvement- and Accountability-oriented Evaluation Approaches](chapter-07-improvement-and-accountability-oriented-evaluation-approaches.qmd)
- [Chapter 8: Social Agenda and Advocacy Evaluation Approaches](chapter-08-social-agenda-and-advocacy-evaluation-approaches.qmd)
- [Chapter 9: Eclectic Evaluation Approaches](chapter-09-eclectic-evaluation-approaches.qmd)
- [Chapter 10: Best Approaches for Twenty-first-century Evaluations](chapter-10-best-approaches-for-twenty-first-century-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 11: Experimental and Quasi-experimental Design Evaluations](chapter-11-experimental-and-quasi-experimental-design-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 12: Case Study Evaluations](chapter-12-case-study-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 13: Daniel Stufflebeam's CIPP Model for Evaluation](chapter-13-daniel-stufflebeams-cipp-model-for-evaluation.qmd)
- [Chapter 14: Michael Scriven's Consumer-oriented Approach to Evaluation](chapter-14-michael-scrivens-consumer-oriented-approach-to-evaluation.qmd)
- [Chapter 15: Robert Stake's Responsive or Stakeholder-centered Evaluation Approach](chapter-15-robert-stakes-responsive-or-stakeholder-centered-evaluation-approach.qmd)
- [Chapter 16: Michael Patton's Utilization-focused Evaluation](chapter-16-michael-pattons-utilization-focused-evaluation.qmd)
- [Chapter 17: Identifying and Assessing Evaluation Opportunities](chapter-17-identifying-and-assessing-evaluation-opportunities.qmd)
- [Chapter 18: First Steps in Addressing Evaluation Opportunities](chapter-18-first-steps-in-addressing-evaluation-opportunities.qmd)
- [Chapter 19: Designing Evaluations](chapter-19-designing-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 20: Budgeting Evaluations](chapter-20-budgeting-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 21: Contracting Evaluations](chapter-21-contracting-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 22: Collecting Evaluative Information](chapter-22-collecting-evaluative-information.qmd)
- [Chapter 23: Analyzing and Synthesizing Information](chapter-23-analyzing-and-synthesizing-information.qmd)
- [Chapter 24: Communicating Evaluation Findings](chapter-24-communicating-evaluation-findings.qmd)
- [Chapter 25: Metaevaluation: Evaluating Evaluations](chapter-25-metaevaluation-evaluating-evaluations.qmd)
- [Chapter 26: Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation](chapter-26-institutionalizing-and-mainstreaming-evaluation.qmd)

## References
